algorithm: {The algorithm selects a promising solution based on the minimum total distance and makespan from the archive, then applies a hybrid local search strategy that involves randomly selecting two routes, merging them while preserving vehicle capacity, and performing a reallocation of customers to minimize distance and makespan, ensuring the new solution remains feasible.}
function:
def select_neighbor(archive: List[Tuple[np.ndarray, Tuple[float, float]]], coords: np.ndarray, demand: np.ndarray, distance_matrix: np.ndarray, capacity: float) -> np.ndarray:
    selected_solution = min(archive, key=lambda x: (x[1][0], x[1][1]))  # Minimize total_distance and makespan
    routes = selected_solution[0]

    # Randomly choose two routes to merge
    if len(routes) < 2:
        return routes  # If there are not enough routes, return as is

    route_indices = random.sample(range(len(routes)), 2)
    route1 = routes[route_indices[0]]
    route2 = routes[route_indices[1]]

    # Attempt to merge route1 and route2
    new_route = []
    new_route.extend(route1[1:-1])  # Avoid depot
    new_route.extend(route2[1:-1])  # Avoid depot

    # Check capacity constraints
    new_demand = sum(demand[i] for i in new_route)
    
    if new_demand > capacity:
        # If merging exceeds capacity, reallocate customers
        new_route = []
        total_demand = 0
        for customer in route1[1:-1]:
            if total_demand + demand[customer] <= capacity:
                new_route.append(customer)
                total_demand += demand[customer]
        for customer in route2[1:-1]:
            if total_demand + demand[customer] <= capacity:
                new_route.append(customer)
                total_demand += demand[customer]

    # Create new routes based on the merged route
    new_routes = []
    new_routes.append(np.array([0] + new_route + [0]))  # Start and end at depot

    # Add remaining routes that were not merged
    for i in range(len(routes)):
        if i not in route_indices:
            new_routes.append(routes[i])

    return new_routes

algorithm: {The new algorithm first selects a promising solution randomly from the archive based on the Pareto front, then identifies a route that has potential for improvement, randomly selects two customers from that route, and attempts to swap their positions while ensuring that the vehicle capacity is not exceeded; if the swap fails or creates an infeasible solution, it will revert to the original and instead try inserting one of the selected customers into a different position within the same route, while also checking for feasibility, and finally, the modified route will be returned as part of a new solution.}
function:
def select_neighbor(archive: List[Tuple[np.ndarray, Tuple[float, float]]], coords: np.ndarray, demand: np.ndarray, distance_matrix: np.ndarray, capacity: float) -> np.ndarray:
    selected_solution, _ = random.choice(archive)
    
    # Create a copy of the selected solution to modify
    new_solution = [np.copy(route) for route in selected_solution]
    
    # Randomly choose one route from the solution
    route_index = random.randint(0, len(new_solution) - 1)
    route = new_solution[route_index]
    
    # Ensure there are at least two customers to swap
    if len(route) <= 2:
        return new_solution  # No change possible

    # Select two random customers from the route (exclude depot)
    customer_indices = list(range(1, len(route) - 1))  # exclude depot at index 0 and the last depot
    idx1, idx2 = random.sample(customer_indices, 2)
    
    # Swap customers and check feasibility
    route[idx1], route[idx2] = route[idx2], route[idx1]
    
    # Calculate the new demand for the modified route
    new_demand = sum(demand[route[1:-1]])  # exclude depot
    if new_demand > capacity:
        # Swap back if it exceeds capacity
        route[idx1], route[idx2] = route[idx2], route[idx1]
        
        # Try inserting one of the customers at a different position
        customer_to_insert = route[idx1]
        route = np.delete(route, idx1)  # Remove the customer from its original position
        
        # Randomly choose a new position to insert
        new_position = random.randint(1, len(route))  # Insert between depot and last customer
        route = np.insert(route, new_position, customer_to_insert)
    
    return new_solution

algorithm: {Select a solution from the archive based on a weighted probability that favors solutions with lower makespan and total distance, then apply a hybrid local search strategy that combines route exchange and demand reassignment by randomly choosing two routes, attempting to exchange customers between them while ensuring that vehicle capacities are respected and that the total distance and makespan are minimized through this intelligent reassignment.}
function:
def select_neighbor(archive: List[Tuple[np.ndarray, Tuple[float, float]]], coords: np.ndarray, demand: np.ndarray, distance_matrix: np.ndarray, capacity: float) -> np.ndarray:
    selected_solution = random.choices(archive, weights=[1/(obj[1]+1) + 1/(obj[0]+1) for _, obj in archive])[0]
    routes = selected_solution[0]
    
    # Randomly select two routes to attempt a customer exchange
    route_indices = list(range(len(routes)))
    route1_idx, route2_idx = random.sample(route_indices, 2)
    
    route1 = routes[route1_idx]
    route2 = routes[route2_idx]
    
    # Randomly select customers from both routes, ensuring we respect capacity
    if len(route1) > 2 and len(route2) > 2:  # Ensure there are customers to exchange
        customer1_idx = random.randint(1, len(route1) - 2)  # Exclude depot
        customer2_idx = random.randint(1, len(route2) - 2)  # Exclude depot

        customer1 = route1[customer1_idx]
        customer2 = route2[customer2_idx]

        # Calculate the new demand sums after the exchange
        new_route1_demand = demand[customer1] - demand[customer2]
        new_route2_demand = demand[customer2] - demand[customer1]

        if new_route1_demand <= capacity and new_route2_demand <= capacity:
            # Perform the exchange
            new_route1 = np.delete(route1, customer1_idx)  # Remove customer1 from route1
            new_route2 = np.delete(route2, customer2_idx)  # Remove customer2 from route2
            
            new_route1 = np.insert(new_route1, -1, customer2)  # Insert customer2 into route1
            new_route2 = np.insert(new_route2, -1, customer1)  # Insert customer1 into route2
            
            # Create new solution
            new_routes = routes.copy()
            new_routes[route1_idx] = new_route1
            new_routes[route2_idx] = new_route2
            
            return new_routes
    
    return routes  # Return original if no valid exchange was made

algorithm: {The new algorithm involves selecting a promising solution from the archive based on the combined performance of both objectives, randomly selecting a route from that solution, applying a hybrid local search by removing a customer from the selected route and attempting to insert it into a different route, ensuring that the vehicle capacities remain respected, and then returning the newly generated neighbor solution.}

def select_neighbor(archive: List[Tuple[np.ndarray, Tuple[float, float]]], coords: np.ndarray, demand: np.ndarray, distance_matrix: np.ndarray, capacity: float) -> np.ndarray:
    solution, objectives = random.choices(archive, weights=[1/(obj[0]+1e-5) + 1/(obj[1]+1e-5) for _, obj in archive], k=1)[0]
    
    # Step 2: Randomly select a route from the chosen solution
    route_index = random.randint(0, len(solution) - 1)
    selected_route = solution[route_index]
    
    # Step 3: Randomly select a customer from the selected route to remove
    if len(selected_route) <= 2:  # Only depot in route
        return solution  # No feasible neighbor can be generated
    
    customer_to_move_index = random.randint(1, len(selected_route) - 2)  # Exclude depot
    customer_to_move = selected_route[customer_to_move_index]
    
    # Step 4: Create a new route without the selected customer
    new_route = np.delete(selected_route, customer_to_move_index)
    
    # Step 5: Try to insert the removed customer into another route
    new_solution = list(solution)
    new_solution[route_index] = new_route
    
    # Step 6: Attempt to insert the customer into a different route respecting the capacity constraint
    insert_success = False
    for i in range(len(new_solution)):
        if i != route_index:  # Don't insert back into the same route
            current_route = new_solution[i]
            total_demand = np.sum(demand[current_route])
            if total_demand + demand[customer_to_move] <= capacity:
                # Attempt to insert the customer in all possible positions in the new route
                for insert_pos in range(1, len(current_route)):  # Exclude depot positions
                    if (current_route[insert_pos - 1] == 0) or (current_route[insert_pos] == 0):  # Cannot insert adjacent to depot
                        continue
                    # Create a new route with the customer inserted
                    temp_route = np.insert(current_route, insert_pos, customer_to_move)
                    if np.sum(demand[temp_route]) <= capacity:
                        new_solution[i] = temp_route
                        insert_success = True
                        break
            if insert_success:
                break
    
    # If insertion was successful, return the new neighbor solution
    return new_solution

algorithm: {The new algorithm involves selecting a solution from the archive based on a random pick, then performing a local search that utilizes a combination of customer removal and reallocation across routes while ensuring that the total demand does not exceed vehicle capacity, employing a strategy where we not only remove a customer from a random route but also attempt to distribute it to other routes based on their remaining capacity in a way that maintains feasibility and attempts to balance the routes' lengths.}

def select_neighbor(archive: List[Tuple[np.ndarray, Tuple[float, float]]], coords: np.ndarray, demand: np.ndarray, distance_matrix: np.ndarray, capacity: float) -> np.ndarray:
    """
    Select a promising solution from the archive and generate a neighbor solution from it.
    Args:
        archive: A list of tuples, where each tuple contains:
            - solution: A list of numpy arrays, each representing a vehicle route. 
                        Each route starts and ends at the depot (node index 0), e.g., [0, 3, 5, 0].
            - objective: A tuple of two float values (total_distance, makespan), 
                        representing the two objective values of the solution.
        
        coords: A numpy array of shape (n_nodes, 2), representing (x, y) coordinates of each node (depot + customers).
        demand: A numpy array of shape (n_nodes,), where demand[i] is the demand of node i. The depot has demand 0.
        distance_matrix: A numpy array of shape (n_nodes, n_nodes), where [i][j] is the Euclidean distance between node i and j.
        capacity: A float representing the maximum capacity of each vehicle.

    Returns:
        A new neighbor solution.
    """
    selected_solution = random.choice(archive)
    routes = selected_solution[0]
    
    # Create a copy of the routes for modification
    neighbor_solution = [np.copy(route) for route in routes]
    
    # Randomly select a route to modify
    route_index = random.randint(0, len(neighbor_solution) - 1)
    selected_route = neighbor_solution[route_index]
    
    if len(selected_route) <= 2:  # Only depot in route
        return neighbor_solution  # No feasible neighbor can be generated
    
    # Randomly select a customer from the selected route to remove
    customer_to_move_index = random.randint(1, len(selected_route) - 2)  # Exclude depot
    customer_to_move = selected_route[customer_to_move_index]
    
    # Create a new route without the selected customer
    new_route = np.delete(selected_route, customer_to_move_index)
    neighbor_solution[route_index] = new_route
    
    # Try to insert the customer into another route
    insert_success = False
    for i in range(len(neighbor_solution)):
        if i != route_index:  # Don't insert back into the same route
            current_route = neighbor_solution[i]
            total_demand = np.sum(demand[current_route])
            if total_demand + demand[customer_to_move] <= capacity:
                # Attempt to insert the customer in all possible positions in the new route
                for insert_pos in range(1, len(current_route)):  # Exclude depot positions
                    if (current_route[insert_pos - 1] == 0) or (current_route[insert_pos] == 0):  # Cannot insert adjacent to depot
                        continue
                    # Create a new route with the customer inserted
                    temp_route = np.insert(current_route, insert_pos, customer_to_move)
                    if np.sum(demand[temp_route]) <= capacity:
                        neighbor_solution[i] = temp_route
                        insert_success = True
                        break
            if insert_success:
                break
    
    return neighbor_solution

algorithm: {Select a solution from the archive based on a random weighted selection favoring lower total distance, then randomly choose a route and identify a customer to relocate; search for an adjacent customer in a different route to swap with, ensuring that the overall demand in both routes does not exceed the vehicle capacity, and if a valid swap is found that improves either objective, accept it as the neighbor solution.}

def select_neighbor(archive: List[Tuple[np.ndarray, Tuple[float, float]]], coords: np.ndarray, demand: np.ndarray, distance_matrix: np.ndarray, capacity: float) -> np.ndarray:
    """
    Select a promising solution from the archive and generate a neighbor solution from it.
    Args:
        archive: A list of tuples, where each tuple contains:
            - solution: A list of numpy arrays, each representing a vehicle route. 
                        Each route starts and ends at the depot (node index 0), e.g., [0, 3, 5, 0].
            - objective: A tuple of two float values (total_distance, makespan), 
                        representing the two objective values of the solution.
        
        coords: A numpy array of shape (n_nodes, 2), representing (x, y) coordinates of each node (depot + customers).
        demand: A numpy array of shape (n_nodes,), where demand[i] is the demand of node i. The depot has demand 0.
        distance_matrix: A numpy array of shape (n_nodes, n_nodes), where [i][j] is the Euclidean distance between node i and j.
        capacity: A float representing the maximum capacity of each vehicle.

    Returns:
        A new neighbor solution.
    """
    weights = [1 / (obj[0] + 1e-5) for _, obj in archive]  # Favor lower total distance
    weights = [w / sum(weights) for w in weights]  # Normalize weights
    selected_index = np.random.choice(len(archive), p=weights)
    selected_solution, (total_distance, makespan) = archive[selected_index]
    
    # Step 2: Randomly select a route and a customer to relocate
    route_index = random.randint(0, len(selected_solution) - 1)
    selected_route = selected_solution[route_index]
    
    if len(selected_route) <= 2:  # Only depot in route
        return selected_solution  # No feasible neighbor can be generated
    
    customer_to_move_index = random.randint(1, len(selected_route) - 2)  # Exclude depot
    customer_to_move = selected_route[customer_to_move_index]
    
    # Step 3: Create a new route without the selected customer
    new_route = np.delete(selected_route, customer_to_move_index)
    
    # Step 4: Attempt to swap with a customer from a different route
    new_solution = list(selected_solution)
    new_solution[route_index] = new_route
    insert_success = False
    
    for i in range(len(new_solution)):
        if i != route_index:  # Don't swap with the same route
            current_route = new_solution[i]
            for j in range(1, len(current_route) - 1):  # Exclude depot
                customer_to_swap = current_route[j]
                
                # Create new routes for both swaps
                temp_route_out = np.delete(current_route, j)  # Remove customer to swap
                temp_route_in = np.insert(new_route, customer_to_move_index, customer_to_swap)  # Insert swapped customer
                
                # Check capacity constraints
                if np.sum(demand[temp_route_out]) <= capacity and np.sum(demand[temp_route_in]) <= capacity:
                    new_solution[i] = temp_route_out
                    new_solution[route_index] = temp_route_in
                    insert_success = True
                    break
            if insert_success:
                break
    
    return new_solution if insert_success else selected_solution

algorithm: {The algorithm begins by randomly selecting a solution from the archive that has a balance between total distance and makespan; then, it identifies a route within that solution and performs a hybrid local search by attempting to swap a pair of nodes in the selected route with nodes from another route while checking for capacity constraints, ensuring the new configuration is feasible, and finally returning the modified solution as a new neighbor solution.}

def select_neighbor(archive: List[Tuple[np.ndarray, Tuple[float, float]]], coords: np.ndarray, demand: np.ndarray, distance_matrix: np.ndarray, capacity: float) -> np.ndarray:
    selected_solution, _ = random.choice(archive)
    
    # Choose one of the routes in the selected solution
    route_index = random.randint(0, len(selected_solution) - 1)
    route = selected_solution[route_index]

    # Attempt to perform a hybrid local search
    new_route = route.copy()
    n = len(route) - 1  # Exclude depot node at start/end
    
    # Randomly select two different nodes to swap within the route
    if n > 2:  # Ensure there are enough nodes to swap
        i, j = random.sample(range(1, n), 2)  # Skip depot
        new_route[i], new_route[j] = new_route[j], new_route[i]  # Swap nodes

        # Validate the new route under capacity constraints
        new_demand = sum(demand[new_route[1:-1]])  # Calculate demand of the new route
        if new_demand <= capacity:
            selected_solution[route_index] = new_route  # Update the route in the solution

    return selected_solution

algorithm: {First, select a promising solution from the archive based on a random weighted selection favoring better objective values, then for the selected solution, identify a route and apply a hybrid local search operator that involves the following: randomly choose two customers from the selected route to swap their positions while maintaining vehicle capacity constraints, if the swap leads to a solution that respects the capacity constraint, then calculate the new total distance and makespan, and if the new solution is better in terms of either objective or both, accept it as the neighbor solution.}
function:
def select_neighbor(archive: List[Tuple[np.ndarray, Tuple[float, float]]], coords: np.ndarray, demand: np.ndarray, distance_matrix: np.ndarray, capacity: float) -> np.ndarray:
    total_objects = len(archive)
    weights = [1 / (1 + obj[1]) for _, obj in archive]  # Favor lower makespan
    weights = [w / sum(weights) for w in weights]  # Normalize weights
    
    selected_index = np.random.choice(total_objects, p=weights)
    selected_solution, (total_distance, makespan) = archive[selected_index]
    
    # Apply hybrid local search operator
    new_solution = [np.copy(route) for route in selected_solution]
    route_index = random.choice(range(len(new_solution)))
    
    if len(new_solution[route_index]) > 3:  # Ensure there are customers to swap
        customer_indices = range(1, len(new_solution[route_index]) - 1)  # Exclude depot
        customer_pair = random.sample(customer_indices, 2)
        
        # Swap customers in the route
        new_solution[route_index][customer_pair[0]], new_solution[route_index][customer_pair[1]] = (
            new_solution[route_index][customer_pair[1]], new_solution[route_index][customer_pair[0]]
        )
        
        # Validate the new solution for capacity constraints
        new_demand = np.zeros(len(new_solution))
        for i, route in enumerate(new_solution):
            for customer in route[1:-1]:  # Exclude depot
                new_demand[i] += demand[customer]
        
        if all(d <= capacity for d in new_demand):
            # Compute new total distance and makespan
            new_total_distance = sum(distance_matrix[route[i], route[i + 1]] for route in new_solution for i in range(len(route) - 1))
            new_makespan = max(sum(distance_matrix[route[i], route[i + 1]] for i in range(len(route) - 1)) for route in new_solution)
            
            # Return the new neighbor solution
            return new_solution

    # If no valid neighbor found, return the original solution
    return selected_solution

algorithm: {Select a promising solution from the archive based on a weighted random approach that considers both objectives, then identify two customer routes from that solution and attempt to merge these routes into a single route while ensuring that the total demand does not exceed the vehicle's capacity; if merging is not feasible, randomly select a route and attempt to add a customer from a different route into it, while checking for feasibility and preserving the route structure.}
function:
def select_neighbor(archive: List[Tuple[np.ndarray, Tuple[float, float]]], coords: np.ndarray, demand: np.ndarray, distance_matrix: np.ndarray, capacity: float) -> np.ndarray:
    """
    Select a promising solution from the archive and generate a neighbor solution from it.
    Args:
        archive: A list of tuples, where each tuple contains:
            - solution: A list of numpy arrays, each representing a vehicle route. 
                        Each route starts and ends at the depot (node index 0), e.g., [0, 3, 5, 0].
            - objective: A tuple of two float values (total_distance, makespan), 
                        representing the two objective values of the solution.
        
        coords: A numpy array of shape (n_nodes, 2), representing (x, y) coordinates of each node (depot + customers).
        demand: A numpy array of shape (n_nodes,), where demand[i] is the demand of node i. The depot has demand 0.
        distance_matrix: A numpy array of shape (n_nodes, n_nodes), where [i][j] is the Euclidean distance between node i and j.
        capacity: A float representing the maximum capacity of each vehicle.

    Returns:
        A new neighbor solution.
    """
    selected_solution, _ = random.choices(archive, weights=[1/(obj[0]+1e-5) + 1/(obj[1]+1e-5) for _, obj in archive], k=1)[0]
    new_solution = [np.copy(route) for route in selected_solution]

    # Randomly select two different routes from the solution
    route_indices = random.sample(range(len(new_solution)), 2)
    route1 = new_solution[route_indices[0]]
    route2 = new_solution[route_indices[1]]

    # Attempt to merge route1 and route2
    combined_route = np.concatenate((route1[:-1], route2[1:]))  # Exclude depot from route2
    if np.sum(demand[combined_route]) <= capacity:
        new_solution[route_indices[0]] = combined_route
        new_solution.pop(route_indices[1])  # Remove the second route
    else:
        # If merging fails, try to insert a customer from route2 into route1
        customer_to_insert_index = random.randint(1, len(route2) - 2)  # Exclude depot
        customer_to_insert = route2[customer_to_insert_index]

        # Remove customer from route2
        route2 = np.delete(route2, customer_to_insert_index)

        # Attempt to insert the customer into route1 at a valid position
        insert_success = False
        for insert_pos in range(1, len(route1)):  # Exclude depot positions
            temp_route = np.insert(route1, insert_pos, customer_to_insert)
            if np.sum(demand[temp_route]) <= capacity:
                new_solution[route_indices[0]] = temp_route
                new_solution[route_indices[1]] = route2  # Keep the modified second route
                insert_success = True
                break

    return new_solution

algorithm: {The algorithm first selects a promising solution from the archive based on a score derived from the objectives, then it randomly selects a route to modify, removes a customer from that route, and attempts to insert that customer into a different route while ensuring feasibility under the vehicle capacity constraint; if the insertion is not feasible, it tries to insert the customer into the original route at different positions until a feasible solution is found, thereby generating a new neighbor solution.}
function:
def select_neighbor(archive: List[Tuple[np.ndarray, Tuple[float, float]]], coords: np.ndarray, demand: np.ndarray, distance_matrix: np.ndarray, capacity: float) -> np.ndarray:
    selected_solution, (total_distance, makespan) = random.choice(archive)
    
    # Randomly select a route from the selected solution
    route_index = random.randint(0, len(selected_solution) - 1)
    selected_route = selected_solution[route_index]
    
    # Randomly select a customer from the selected route (excluding the depot)
    if len(selected_route) <= 2:  # Only depot present
        return selected_solution  # Return the original if no customer to move

    customer_index = random.randint(1, len(selected_route) - 2)  # Exclude depot at both ends
    customer = selected_route[customer_index]
    selected_route = np.delete(selected_route, customer_index)  # Remove the customer from the route
    
    # Calculate the remaining capacity after removing the customer
    remaining_demand = demand[customer]
    
    # Attempt to insert the customer into a different route or the same route while maintaining feasibility
    new_solution = [np.copy(route) for route in selected_solution]  # Deep copy of the solution
    inserted = False
    
    # Try to insert into other routes first
    for i in range(len(new_solution)):
        if i != route_index:  # Skip the original route
            current_route = new_solution[i]
            current_demand = sum(demand[node] for node in current_route)
            
            if current_demand + remaining_demand <= capacity:
                # Insert the customer into the route at a random position
                insert_position = random.randint(1, len(current_route))  # Can insert after depot or at the end
                new_solution[i] = np.insert(current_route, insert_position, customer)
                inserted = True
                break
    
    # If not inserted into a different route, try back to the original route
    if not inserted:
        current_route = new_solution[route_index]
        current_demand = sum(demand[node] for node in current_route)
        
        if current_demand + remaining_demand <= capacity:
            # Insert back into the original route at a random position
            insert_position = random.randint(1, len(current_route))  # Can insert after depot or at the end
            new_solution[route_index] = np.insert(current_route, insert_position, customer)
    
    return new_solution
